eth-etf-institutional.title: Spot Ethereum ETF flows turn positive as institutions return
eth-etf-institutional.summary: Weekly net inflows flipped from negative; long-term holders tick up.
eth-etf-institutional.body:
Major spot Ethereum ETFs posted net inflows last week, ending a multi-week outflow streak. On-chain metrics show a modest rise in long-term holder share and a decline in short-term speculative share.

Analysts link the shift to improved macro liquidity expectations and busier Layer2 activity. US inflation prints and the rate path remain key for risk appetite.

Informational only; not investment advice.

btc-halving-liquidity.title: Post-halving Bitcoin: fees and miner revenue mix under the microscope
btc-halving-liquidity.summary: As block subsidies decline, fees and L2/inscription activity matter more.
btc-halving-liquidity.body:
With subsidies stepping down, fees are becoming a larger part of miner revenue. Lightning and sidechains continue to scale payments and settlement.

Focus is shifting from halving hype to sustainable usage and fee markets. Distinguish cycle narratives from fundamentals.

Data from public on-chain sources and industry research; demo is not live node data.

stablecoin-mica.title: Stablecoin rules: reserves, audits, and redemption transparency
stablecoin-mica.summary: Key jurisdictions stress auditable reserves and clear redemption rails.
stablecoin-mica.body:
Recent drafts require stablecoin issuers to disclose reserve composition and undergo third-party audits. Exchanges may tighten listing standards and risk checklists.

Users should review transparency, redemption paths, and counterparty risk—not yield alone.

Regulatory outcomes are uncertain; follow final law and platform notices.

layer2-dencun.title: Cheaper L2 fees lift activity: DEX and social apps benefit
layer2-dencun.summary: Data-availability improvements cut costs; projects migrate faster.
layer2-dencun.body:
As mainnet upgrades and L2 mature, average costs for DEX and on-chain social use cases have fallen. Developers move more logic to L2.

Exchange wallets and Web3 entry points must improve cross-chain safety, account abstraction, and UX.

Trend summary; individual projects vary by chain and environment.

exchange-proof-reserves.title: Proof of reserves: frequency, merkle snapshots, and third-party audits
exchange-proof-reserves.summary: Major venues publish more often; merkle roots are a common pattern.
exchange-proof-reserves.body:
PoR helps users verify that on-chain assets cover liabilities at a point in time. Typical patterns include periodic merkle root publication, audit reports, and hot/cold address disclosure.

PoR is a point-in-time snapshot and not alone proof of ongoing solvency; combine with governance, risk, and compliance.

Demo does not endorse any venue.

macro-fed-watch.title: Macro calendar: rates and DXY as drivers for risk assets
macro-fed-watch.summary: Inflation and jobs data remain key short-term catalysts.
macro-fed-watch.body:
This week’s prints may move rate expectations. Historically, liquidity regime shifts can raise short-term correlation between crypto and growth equities.

Watch leverage and margin usage around releases.

Macro views are subjective; size positions to your risk tolerance.
